Those are five interlocking injustices that threaten the soul of this nation. And if you don’t heal them—work on them in the body—then the soul, the peace, the domestic tranquility, the promises of the nation, are always being undermined. And you cannot address any of those things without addressing concrete, real policies.

William Barber on whether America can heal, in which he names five interlocking injustices — systemic racism, systemic poverty, ecological devastation, the war economy, and religious nationalism — that must be addressed through concrete policy, not just rhetoric.

title: Raptors, Celtics Players Jointly Discussing Boycott, Other Measures Following Jacob Blake Shooting
note:
From the moment the NBA and the National Basketball Players Association agreed to create the bubble and attempt to restart the season amid the coronavirus pandemic, attempts to address the ongoing issues of police brutality, social justice and racial inequality in American society have been top of mind. The words "Black Lives Matter" are painted on all three game courts at ESPN's Wide World of Sports Complex, and players are wearing "Black Lives Matter" warm-up shirts before every game. The majority of players also are are wearing jerseys with words or phrases that were agreed upon to promote positive change in society.

"So now it's more about what can we do next to not only show that we're not only involved, but also show that we really care and that it saddens us to see nothing's changing. And there's something that has to happen. And that's part of the discussions and creative ways of how we can create change and how we can not only be there for those who are experiencing tough trials, like the Blake family right now, and not only support them, but also show their stories, send their message out to the communities that show how this world is and how we can hopefully get better and improve as a society. Not one by one, but hopefully as a whole."

"Yeah, I mean, how many points we score, that s--- don't matter right now," he said. "Being a Black man in America is more important than what I'm doing out there on a basketball court. Using my platform, my voice to help create conversations and change is more important than anything I can do out there. You think about a man getting shot in his back seven times with his kids in the car is way more important than anything I can do out there on the floor.
blurb: ESPN reporting on NBA players' 2020 boycott discussions after the police shooting of Jacob Blake, capturing athletes using their platform to push for concrete change rather than symbolic gestures alone.

title: New Poll: Mental Health Challenges Prevalent Among Gen Z Youth
blurb: A Blue Shield of California poll finding that more than three in four Gen Z youth have discussed their mental health struggles with someone else, underscoring both prevalence and growing openness.

title: The Gen Z Activism Survey
blurb: A United Way of the Bay Area survey on how Gen Z engages with activism and civic participation -- what motivates them, what discourages them, and where they see themselves making change.

title: Deloitte Global Gen Z and Millennial Survey 2025
blurb: Deloitte's annual global survey of Gen Z and millennial attitudes toward work, money, climate, and mental health, tracked year over year across dozens of countries.

title: Workshop — Eliza Filby
blurb: The workshop offerings page for generational historian Eliza Filby, who advises organizations on navigating generational change and intergenerational tension in the workplace.
ambiguous_note: This is a services/workshop listing page rather than a single piece of content; specific workshop content isn't confirmable from the URL alone.

title: The Tarot Cards of Tech | The power of predicting impact | Artefact
blurb: Artefact's Tarot Cards of Tech is a foresight card deck and framework designed to help teams surface the unintended consequences of a new technology before they build it.

title: Remarkable Experiences Report
blurb: A 2026 report on what makes experiences memorable and meaningful, examined here for what it suggests about designing moments of wonder and connection rather than routine ones.
ambiguous_note: Full authorship and methodology of this report aren't confirmable from the title and file path alone.

title: Empathetic Leadership Can Make or Break AI Adoption
blurb: A Harvard Business Review piece arguing that how empathetically leaders roll out AI tools -- not just the tools themselves -- determines whether employees trust and adopt them.

title: From Fragmentation to Framework How IMAGINE Is Helping a Field Find Itself
blurb: A Medium essay on how the IMAGINE initiative is trying to give a fragmented field a shared framework and common language for its work.
ambiguous_note: What field and what IMAGINE refers to specifically aren't confirmable from the title alone.

title: I asked 100 young people about the AI future. Here's what they told me
blurb: An SF Chronicle opinion piece reporting on conversations with 100 young people about how they see AI shaping their futures -- their hopes, anxieties, and expectations.

title: Evidence for the future? Strategic foresight as a source of evidence for policymaking...
blurb: A paper examining whether and how strategic foresight work can be treated as legitimate evidence within policymaking processes that are usually built around retrospective data.
ambiguous_note: Hosted as a shared LinkedIn document; full text not independently verified beyond the title.

title: Development and application of the Masculinity Content Classification Framework
blurb: An academic paper presenting a framework for classifying media content according to the models of masculinity it portrays, developed for use in gender-focused media research.
ambiguous_note: Hosted as a Google Drive document; full methodology not independently verified beyond the title.

title: Mapping futures literacy as a competence space...
blurb: A Springer journal article that maps futures literacy -- the capacity to imagine and use the future -- as a structured space of competencies, building on UNESCO's futures literacy framework.
